notes: 4.1.4.2 breaking development line: TaskContext and ContextReader own progressive disclosure; TaskWorkspace owns task files; RecordStore owns records and durability; SkillLibrary owns immutable installed Skill revisions; AgentExecution owns task-scoped Skill binding. AgentTask process state stays in memory/logs by default and record_store_recovery is opt-in. The former combined Workspace, ContextBuilder, SkillsManager, Skills execution route/strategies, skill_activation, and workspace_operation are removed without shims.

runtime_event_ownership.plugin_contract: plugins return observations/errors/decisions; core maps them to official RuntimeEvent records

runtime_control.record_store_contract: RecordStore may persist canonical RuntimeEvent records when explicitly bound as runtime_event_store; TaskWorkspace is never an event store.
runtime_control.task_context_contract: ContextPackage and ContextConsumption projections are bounded observation facts only and never drive route selection, verification, or task acceptance.
runtime_control.model_request_telemetry_contract: Existing model RuntimeEvents may carry payload.model_request_telemetry observation facts; telemetry remains observation-only.
runtime_control.model_request_result_stream_status_contr: ModelRequestResult reserves $status for completed, failed, and cancelled outcomes.

runtime_contract.selection_and_binding_owner: AgentExecution structured semantic selection with host-issued keys and fail-closed validation
runtime_contract.disclosure_owner: TaskContext plus SkillContextSource plus consumer-bound ContextReader
runtime_contract.execution_policy: No Skills route, Skill-local strategy, stage engine, script actionization, capability inference, or capability mounting
runtime_contract.revision_binding_event: skills.revisions.bound records exact revision availability without claiming activation
runtime_contract.context_consumption_event: skills.context.bound records concrete ModelRequest response-bound context consumption; availability alone is not consumption or Action evidence
runtime_contract.remote_source_policy: Authorized host code materializes remote sources before local SkillLibrary installation

blocks.lifecycle_contract: Blocks lowers validated ExecutionPlan data to TriggerFlow-backed ExecutionBlockGraph and maps results/evidence; it is not a Skill engine or persistence owner.
blocks.context_read_contract: context_read consumes one caller-bound ContextReader and permits read/search/scoped_search only.
blocks.task_dag_contract: TaskDAGExecutor validates submitted DAG data before direct TriggerFlow execution; compile_blocks and async_run_blocks are explicit opt-in carriers.

action_runtime.task_workspace_contract: TaskWorkspace Actions own bounded file read/search/write/edit/patch/export operations.
action_runtime.record_store_contract: Action persistence is explicit and does not follow from a TaskWorkspace binding.

triggerflow.task_workspace_contract: TriggerFlow does not create or infer a TaskWorkspace.
triggerflow.durability_contract: RecordStore or another explicit provider supplies snapshot, runtime-event, lease, and artifact-ref ports.

task_context.selection_contract: Required and explicit blocks cannot be dropped; optional prose relevance uses ModelRequest structured output and host-issued keys.

task_workspace.ownership: Task files, path policy, physical readback, digest, and file refs

record_store.ownership: Records, indexes, links, checkpoints, snapshots, runtime events, leases, and memory persistence

agent_execution_request_scope.contract: Each call owns an isolated AgentExecution draft. Completed executions are immutable run records; prompt/config mutation after start fails fast.

agent_execution_task_loop.context_contract: AgentExecution and AgentTask share one TaskContext and one execution-scoped TaskWorkspace view.
agent_execution_task_loop.durability_contract: Process state stays in memory/logs by default; record_store_recovery is opt-in.

skills.contract: Direct AgentExecution binding is canonical; async_read_task_context binds consumer/phase and accepts an optional string or ContextReadIntent override; run_skills_task is a result-shaped adapter and Agently.skills_executor is management/context compatibility only.

public_typing.contract: New public methods default to typed parameters and returns; Any boundaries require explicit allowlist reasons.
public_typing.compatibility_policy: The allowlist records deliberate Any boundaries; it is not a public-method allowlist.
